After the cataclysmic events in New York with her fellow Furvengers in 2012, Brenna "Brenda" Prrfler (aka Captain Prrfler), an anthro female North American cougar, lives in the nation's capital as he tries to adjust to modern times after being thawed out nearly 70 years after the end of World War II. An attack on S.H.I.E.L.D. throws Rogers into a web of intrigue that places the whole world at risk. Joining forces with the Black Vixen and a new ally, the Falcon, Brenda struggles to expose an ever-widening conspiracy that compromises all of S.H.I.E.L.D., but she and her team soon come up against an unexpected enemy.

✴️ *Updated to go along with the continuity of the Furvengers RPG* ✴️

===================

Nearly 70 years after the end of World War II and 2 years after the Battle of New York, Brenna "Brenda" Prrfler, an anthro female North American cougar, struggles to acclimate to modern society in Washington, D.C. She's working as a S.H.I.E.L.D. agent, carrying out missions alongside Cya-Chan, aka the Black Vixen, and her new friend Sam Wilson, a former Air Force veteran. Brenda keeps a notebook of things she needs to catch up on, ranging from the 1969 Moon Landing to Thai food.

On a mission to rescue hostages from a hijacked S.H.I.E.L.D. vessel, the Lemurian Star, Brenda leads an elite team called STRIKE, including Cye-Chan. During the mission, Brenda notices Cye has a separate objective: retrieving data from the ship's computers—a task given to her by Nikki Fury, the anthro female wolf herself. Steve confronts Fury about the deception, which strains their relationship. Fury insists that the data is crucial to S.H.I.E.L.D.'s top-secret project, "Project Insight". This initiative involves three next-generation Helicarriers designed to neutralize threats before they even occur, using a satellite-linked targeting system. Brenda expresses her discomfort with the project's preemptive nature, believing it to be a form of global control, not protection.
